MASTER SERVICE AND TECHNOLOGY AGREEMENT\n Confidential – Fully Executed\n \n Prevail InfoWorks, Inc. ("Prevail") and BriaCell Therapeutics Corp. ("Company")\n Effective Date: May 9, 2023\n Source: SEC EDGAR – 8-K, Exhibit 10.1, Period 2023-05-09, Filed 2023-05-15 | BriaCell Therapeutics Corp. (BCTXZ) | CIK 0001610820\n | Public Record\n \nPARTIES\n(1) Prevail InfoWorks, Inc. is a corporation having its principal place of business at 211 North 13th Street, Suite 600,\nPhiladelphia, PA 19107-1610, USA ("Prevail"); and\n(2) BriaCell Therapeutics Corp. is a corporation having its principal place of business at 2929 Arch Street, 3rd Floor,\nPhiladelphia, PA 19104 ("Company").\n(3) Prevail and Company may be individually referred to as a "Party" or collectively as the "Parties."\nBACKGROUND\n(1) Prevail provides clinical research services to life science companies and has developed certain software applications\nand platforms, which it makes available as a service to customers via the Internet for a fee for the purpose of managing\nthe customers' clinical trials and analyzing the data from them.\n(2) Company wishes to use Prevail's services and software in its business operations.\n(3) Prevail has agreed to provide and Company has agreed to use and pay for Prevail's services and technologies subject\nto the terms and conditions of this Agreement.\n1. DEFINITIONS\nApplicable Laws: all supranational, national, state, and local laws and regulations including, without limitation, the\nregulations and guidelines of the United States Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 301, et. seq.) (the "FDA\nAct"), the Canadian Food and Drugs Act, and all International Conference on Harmonization (ICH) guidance documents\nincluding ICH E6: Guidelines on Good Clinical Practice (ICH/GCP).\nCompany Data: the data generated in the clinical trial or trials referenced in an executed SOW, as well as data imported\nor inputted by Company, Authorized Users, or Prevail or others on Company's behalf in connection with the Services.\nAuthorized Users: those officers, directors, employees, agents, and independent contractors of Company who are\nauthorized by Company to use the Services and the Documentation.\nConfidential Information: is defined in clause 11.\nDebarment Act: the Generic Drug Enforcement Act of 1992, as amended, 21 U.S.C. §§ 306.\nDocumentation: the documents made available to Company by Prevail from time to time which sets out a description of\nthe Services and the user instructions for the Services, including any executed SOWs.\nInitial Term: the initial term of this Agreement is 28 months.\n\nNormal Business Hours: 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time, USA, each Business Day.\nServices: the services provided by Prevail to Company under this Agreement as set forth in a Statement of Work attached\nhereto and any other executed Statements of Work.\nSoftware: the online software applications provided by Prevail as part of the Services.\nStudy Invention: any trade secrets, discovery, invention, technology, copyright, know-how, method, result, data, material,\ninformation, concept, processes, works, formulas, source and object codes, programs, other works of authorship,\nimprovements, developments, designs, or techniques, whether or not patentable, that Company or Prevail or their\nrespective employees, agents, and/or subcontractors, either alone or jointly with others, makes, conceives, creates,\ndevelops, or reduces to practice as a result of conducting a Study or performing obligations under this Agreement.\nSubscription Fees: the subscription fees payable by Company to Prevail for the Software and Services, as set forth in\nSchedule 1 attached hereto and any other executed SOWs.\nUser Subscriptions: the user subscriptions purchased by Company pursuant to clause 5 which entitle Authorized Users to\naccess and use the Services and the Documentation in accordance with this Agreement.\n2. SCOPE OF SERVICES\nDuring the Initial Term and any Renewal Period, and from time to time, Company may purchase Services from Prevail\nthat will be set forth in a Statement of Work ("SOW") agreed by and duly executed by both Parties, beginning with SOW 1\nattached hereto. Each executed SOW shall be governed by this Agreement and include: (i) description of Services to be\nprovided, (ii) fee and payment schedule for the Services and a reasonably detailed budget, (iii) description of deliverables\nto be delivered by Prevail and their intended use, and (iv) a project timeline.\nAny changes or modifications to the SOW shall be mutually agreed upon in writing by the Parties as provided for in this\nAgreement. Once executed by both Parties, each SOW shall be deemed incorporated into this Agreement by reference.\nIn the event that the terms of an executed SOW conflict with the terms of this Agreement, the terms of this Agreement\nshall govern unless the SOW specifically references Section 2 of this Agreement and indicates such specific terms of the\nSOW shall govern.\n3. CHANGES TO SCOPE OF SERVICES\nIn the event that Company would like Prevail to alter the Services under a given SOW or the assumptions underlying the\nbudget, the Parties shall agree upon a signed written amendment to the SOW prior to the provision of said Services.\nPrevail has no obligation to perform and Company has no obligation to pay for any additional or modified Services absent\nwritten amendment to the SOW by the Parties with respect thereto.\n5. TECHNOLOGY USER SUBSCRIPTIONS\nSubject to terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement and SOWs duly signed by both Parties (including the attached\nSOW 1), Prevail hereby grants to Company a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to permit Authorized Users to use the\nServices and the Documentation during the Term solely for Company's product research, development and\ncommercialization and business operations. Company may, from time to time during any Subscription Term, purchase\nadditional User Subscriptions in excess of the number set out in a SOW and Prevail shall grant access to the Services\nand the Documentation to such additional Authorized Users in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.\n6. COMPANY DATA\n\n6.1 As between Company and Prevail, Company shall solely own all rights, title, and interest in and to all of Company\nData and shall have sole responsibility for the legality, reliability, integrity, accuracy and quality of Company Data.\n6.2 Company Data will be hosted, under Prevail's responsibility, in a secure data center certified as ISO/IEC 27001:2013\nand SOC 2 compliant. Prevail shall follow its archiving procedures for Company Data which include hosting Company\nData at another location in a second secure data center. Prevail shall store Company Data in Canada or in the USA.\n6.3 Prevail will ensure that no Company Data is used for any purposes whatsoever other than to provide the Services\nhereunder.\n7. PREVAIL'S OBLIGATIONS\n7.1 Prevail shall, during the Term, provide the Services and make available the Documentation to Company subject to the\nterms of this Agreement, Applicable Laws, and Prevail's applicable Standard Operating Procedures unless other SOPs\nare identified within a SOW.\n7.2 Prevail undertakes that the Services will be performed substantially in accordance with the Documentation and with\nreasonable skill and care.\n7.3 Prevail represents and warrants that it has not been debarred and has not been convicted of a crime which could lead\nto debarment under the Debarment Act. In the event that Prevail or any of its officers, directors, or employees becomes\ndebarred, Prevail shall notify Company immediately in writing.\n7.4 Prevail shall make the Software available in accordance with the Availability service level: the Software Availability for\nany given month shall be at least 99.9% ("Availability") uptime. In the event that Prevail does not meet the Availability for\na given month, Prevail will provide a Service Credit to Company equal to: 5% of the prior month's Subscription Fees for\nAvailability between 98.0%-99.89%; 10% for Availability between 95.0%-97.9%; and 25% for Availability below 95.0%.\n7.5 Prevail will, as part of the Services and at no additional cost to Company, provide Company with Prevail's standard\ncustomer support services including phone support for Authorized Users on a 24/7/365 basis, with Tier 1 support staff\nwho can escalate to Tier 2 support when needed during Normal Business Hours.\n7.6 Priority Level Definitions and Response Times: Critical (completely prevents operation of Software, no work-around):\nInitial Response 15 minutes, Status Updates every hour, Targeted Resolution within 4 hours. Priority (substantially\nrestricts operations, no work-around): Initial Response 30 minutes, Status Updates every 4 hours, Targeted Resolution\nwithin 8 Business Hours. General (does not substantially restrict operations): Initial Response 60 minutes, Status Updates\nevery 24 hours, Targeted Resolution within 48 hours.\n7.7 Prevail represents and warrants that it has implemented, uses, and will maintain: (a) security measures consistent\nwith up-to-date industry standards to defend against viruses, malware, and unauthorized access; (b) appropriate technical\nand organizational measures and internal controls to protect Company Data against accidental loss, destruction, or\nalteration; (c) data recovery procedures, including regular and multiple copies of Company Data in different places; (d)\nreviews of data recovery procedures at least every twelve months; and (e) measures aimed at protecting Company Data\nagainst all unlawful forms of processing.\n7.8 Prevail represents and warrants that, to the extent applicable, it shall comply with HIPAA with respect to protected\nhealth information, and any other applicable data privacy and protection laws relevant to the Services. Prevail shall limit\nits use, onward transfer, and further disclosure of any protected personal information to only those activities specified in\nthis Agreement.\n7.9 If the Services will involve the collection or processing of personal data within the European Union, then Company (or\nits designee) shall serve as the Data Controller and Prevail shall serve as the Data Processor, and Prevail shall act only\nunder the instructions of Company in regard to such personal data in compliance with GDPR.\n\n8. COMPANY'S OBLIGATIONS\nCompany shall: (a) comply with all Applicable Laws; (b) remain responsible for any regulatory obligations not specifically\nand expressly transferred to Prevail under the applicable SOW; (c) provide Prevail with reasonably necessary\nco-operation in relation to this Agreement, including access to such information as may be reasonably necessary for\nPrevail to render the Services; and (d) be solely responsible for procuring and maintaining its network connections and\ntelecommunications links from its systems to Prevail's data centers.\n9. FEES AND PAYMENT\nCompany shall pay the Fees to Prevail for the Services and Software in accordance with the executed SOW(s).\n10. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY\n10.1 Company owns all right, title, and interest in and to any Study Inventions, regardless of the Party that makes,\ndiscovers, creates, or invents such Study Invention. Prevail hereby irrevocably and unconditionally assigns, and agrees to\nassign, to Company all of its right, title, and interest in and to all such Study Inventions including those that are made,\nconceived, created, developed, or reduced to practice by Prevail, any of its employees or permitted subcontractors, and\nany related patent claims and other intellectual property rights.\n10.2 Company acknowledges and agrees that Prevail and/or its licensors own all intellectual property rights in the\nServices and the Documentation. Except as expressly stated herein, this Agreement does not grant Company any rights\nto, or in, patents, copyrights, database rights, trade secrets, trade names, trademarks (whether registered or\nunregistered), or any other rights or licenses in respect to the Services or the Documentation.\n10.3 Prevail represents and warrants that it has all the rights in relation to the Software, Services and the Documentation\nthat are necessary to grant all the rights it purports to grant under this Agreement, and that the Software, Services, and\nDocumentation, and Company's use thereof, shall not infringe or misappropriate the intellectual property of any third\nparty.\n11. CONFIDENTIALITY\n"Confidential Information" shall mean any non-public information relating to the business, operations and activities of a\nParty, whether or not marked or identified as confidential or proprietary, which the receiving Party should reasonably\nunderstand to be confidential information under the circumstances, including know-how, trade secrets and, in the case of\nCompany, Company Data and Study Inventions.\nEach Party shall (i) maintain the confidentiality of the other Party's Confidential Information, using at a minimum the same\nsafeguards afforded its own confidential, proprietary trade secrets, but in no event less than reasonable care; (ii) use the\nother Party's Confidential Information only to the extent required for the performance of this Agreement; (iii) restrict\ndisclosure and access to the other Party's Confidential Information only to its employees, suppliers, permitted\nsubcontractors, advisors and consultants who have a need to know for the performance of this Agreement, provided that\nthey are bound by confidentiality and restricted use obligations no less restrictive than those contained herein; and (iv) not\ndisclose, provide, transfer, rent, sublicense, or otherwise make available any portion of the other Party's Confidential\nInformation to any third party.\nThe non-disclosure and restricted use obligations shall not apply to information which the receiving Party can prove: (i) is\nnow or hereafter known to the public through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) was in the possession of the receiving\nParty prior to disclosure by the disclosing Party; (iii) has been properly obtained without restriction from a third party who\nis not bound by an obligation of confidentiality; (iv) is independently developed by the receiving Party without reference to\n\nor use of the disclosing Party's Confidential Information; or (v) is disclosed by court order or as otherwise required by law,\nprovided that the Party required to disclose provides prompt advance notice to enable the other Party to seek a protective\norder. The obligations in this clause 11 shall continue indefinitely after the expiration or termination of this Agreement.\n12. INDEMNIFICATION AND LIMITATION OF LIABILITY\n12.1 Prevail shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Company and its affiliates, and their respective employees,\nofficers, directors, agents, assigns and successors-in-interest from and against any and all losses, damages, liabilities,\ncosts (including reasonable legal fees) incurred by an Indemnified Party resulting from any third-party claim caused by (i)\nthe Software, the Services, any deliverables to the extent provided by Prevail, infringing or misappropriating such third\nparty's intellectual property rights, and (ii) the use of the Company Data in a manner not authorized by this Agreement.\n12.2 NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, PUNITIVE, OR\nCONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, OR ANY LOSS OF REVENUE OR PROFITS (EXCLUDING FEES UNDER THE\nAGREEMENT), DATA, OR DATA USE. PREVAIL'S MAXIMUM LIABILITY FOR ANY DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR\nRELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, THE SOFTWARE OR SERVICES, WHETHER IN CONTRACT OR TORT, OR\nOTHERWISE, SHALL IN NO EVENT EXCEED, IN THE AGGREGATE, THE TOTAL AMOUNTS ACTUALLY PAID TO\nPREVAIL FOR THE SOFTWARE AND SERVICES UNDER THIS AGREEMENT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE\nCLAIM IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTH PERIOD IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO SUCH\nCLAIM.\n13. INSURANCE\nEach Party shall maintain in effect during the Term of this Agreement adequate forms and levels of insurance as may\nreasonably be expected by a going concern in the jurisdictions in which it conducts business.\n14. TERM AND TERMINATION\n14.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for the Initial Term (28 months) and,\nthereafter, this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive periods of 12 months (each a Renewal Period),\nunless: (a) either Party notifies the other Party of termination, in writing, at least 60 days before the end of the Initial Term\nor any Renewal Period; or (b) otherwise terminated in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.\n14.2 Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies to which the parties may be entitled, either Party may terminate this\nAgreement without liability to the other if the other Party commits a material breach of any of the terms of this Agreement\nand (if such a breach is remediable) fails to remedy that breach within 30 days of that Party being notified in writing of the\nbreach.\n14.3 Company may terminate this Agreement, or any SOW (or a portion thereof) at any time prior to completion by giving\n30 days prior written notice to Prevail. Prevail shall promptly comply with the terms of such notice(s) to terminate work on\nthe SOW and use its best efforts to limit any further cost to Company.\n14.4 On termination of this Agreement for any reason: (a) Company shall pay Prevail all direct expenses and fees for\nServices completed and costs incurred or irrevocably obligated as of the date of termination; (b) all licenses granted\nunder this Agreement shall immediately terminate; (c) each Party shall return and make no further use of any equipment,\nproperty, Documentation, and other items belonging to the other Party; and (d) Company shall provide Prevail written\ninstructions within 30 days to either return Company Data or dispose of Company Data at Company's reasonable\nexpense.\n\n15. ENTIRE AGREEMENT AND COUNTERPARTS\nThis Agreement, and any documents referred to in it, constitute the whole agreement between the parties and supersede\nany previous arrangement, understanding or agreement between them relating to the subject matter they cover.\nThis Agreement (including its SOWs) may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which together shall\nconstitute a single document. This Agreement and its SOWs may be executed by facsimile or digital file showing a Party's\nsignature (e.g., a .pdf). Such facsimile or digital file shall be deemed an original and fully enforceable and admissible in\nany legal proceeding.\n16. MODIFICATION AND ASSIGNMENT\nNo supplement or modification of this Agreement shall be binding unless executed in writing by both parties. Neither Party\nshall, without the prior written consent of the other Party, assign, transfer, charge, or sub-contract all or any of its rights or\nobligations under this Agreement; provided that either Party may assign its rights and delegate its duties under this\nAgreement to an affiliate or to a successor to such Party's business by merger, acquisition, or purchase of the business.\n17. GOVERNING LAW\nThis Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, U.S.A. without regard to the\nconflicts of law provisions thereof.\n18. NOTICES\nAny notice required to be given under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand or overnight\ndelivery to the other Party at its address set out in this Agreement, or such other address as may have been notified by\nthat Party for such purposes.\nEXECUTED SIGNATURE PAGE\nThis Agreement has been entered into on the date stated at the beginning of it.\nBRIACELL THERAPEUTICS CORP.\nBy: /s/ William V. Williams\nName: William V. Williams\nTitle: President and CEO\nDate: May 9, 2023\nPREVAIL INFOWORKS, INC.\nBy: /s/ Jack Houriet\nName: Jack Houriet\nTitle: CEO\nDate: May 9, 2023\n
